riskFactors: poor genital hygiene and tight clothing (association)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms plus microscopy when available; pH usually normal; culture for recurrent/complicated cases

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; recurrence common when diabetes is uncontrolled

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often clinical diagnosis; limited microscopy in peripheral settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical azoles or oral fluconazole; longer maintenance for recurrent VVC; address diabetes and triggers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Misdiagnosis and overuse of antifungals without confirmation; limited lab access
